document.title: Product Requirements Document: Alloy Framework TypeSpec Emitter

product_overview.name: @microsoft/http-client-js
product_overview.description: A TypeSpec-to-TypeScript code emitter built on the Alloy Framework that generates modular TypeScript client SDKs for REST services. This is a ground-up rewrite of the legacy autorest.typescript/typespec-ts emitter, replacing ts-morph imperative AST manipulation with declarative Alloy JSX components.
product_overview.problem_statement: The legacy emitter (autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts) suffers from: (1) overly complex code using raw string concatenation and manual import management, (2) fragile string-based reference resolution causing self-import bugs, (3) monolithic helper files (2700+ lines) that are hard to maintain, (4) no framework for symbol tracking or cross-file reference management. These issues increase maintenance cost, slow feature development, and introduce subtle bugs.
product_overview.solution: Rewrite the emitter using Alloy Framework JSX components with automatic symbol resolution, refkey-based cross-file references, and composable component architecture. The rewrite must produce output-identical TypeScript SDKs to prevent breaking changes for existing Azure SDK consumers.
target_users: TypeSpec SDK authors generating TypeScript client libraries
Azure SDK team generating Azure service client SDKs
Internal Microsoft teams generating REST client code from TypeSpec definitions

goals.title: Output Parity
goals.description: Generated TypeScript SDK code must be functionally identical to the legacy emitter output. The public API surface (exported types, function signatures, class shapes) must not change.
goals.success_metric: All 101 scenario tests pass with matching output (modulo whitespace and import ordering)

goals.title: Pure Alloy Architecture
goals.description: The entire emitter must use idiomatic Alloy patterns: JSX components, refkeys for all declarations/references, code tagged templates, context providers. No legacy bridges, no string concatenation for imports, no manual path calculation.
goals.success_metric: Zero instances of manual import string construction or string-based reference resolution in codebase

goals.title: Composable Azure Extension
goals.description: Core emitter works standalone for non-Azure scenarios. Azure-specific features (auth, logging, polling, cloud settings) are added via JSX composition, not conditional flags.
goals.success_metric: Core emitter generates valid non-Azure SDK; Azure wrapper adds Azure-specific features without modifying core components

goals.title: Maintainability
goals.description: Dramatically reduce code complexity compared to legacy emitter. Each component has single responsibility, is independently testable, and is under 300 lines.
goals.success_metric: No component file exceeds 300 lines; each component has at least one test

goals.title: Developer Experience
goals.description: New components are easy to add and test. The component architecture makes it straightforward to extend the emitter for new features.
goals.success_metric: Adding a new feature (e.g., new serialization format) requires creating a new component, not modifying existing ones

non_goals.title: RLC (Rest Level Client) Rewrite
non_goals.description: The RLC code path will NOT be ported. Only the modular path is being rewritten.

non_goals.title: typespec-test Package
non_goals.description: The integration test package (typespec-test) will not be ported in this phase.

non_goals.title: Behavioral Changes
non_goals.description: This rewrite must NOT introduce behavioral changes to generated code. Output parity is the primary constraint.

non_goals.title: New Features
non_goals.description: No new code generation features will be added during the rewrite. Feature parity only.

non_goals.title: Runtime Library Changes
non_goals.description: No changes to @typespec/ts-http-runtime or Azure runtime packages.

functional.title: TypeSpec Emitter Integration
functional.description: Implement $onEmit hook that receives TypeSpec EmitContext, initializes TCGC SdkContext, builds Alloy JSX component tree, and writes output files.
acceptance_criteria: Emitter registers via TypeSpec lib definition
Extracts SdkPackage with clients, models, enums
Emits TypeScript files to configured output directory
functional.implementation_notes: Emitter entry point ($onEmit) implemented in emitter.tsx. Registers with TypeSpec compiler, calls createSdkContext(), builds JSX tree, renders via writeOutput().

functional.title: Model Type Generation
functional.description: Generate TypeScript interfaces from SdkModelType, including properties, inheritance, discriminators, additional properties, and documentation.
acceptance_criteria: JSDoc documentation from SDK model doc/summary

functional.title: Enum Type Generation
functional.description: Generate TypeScript enums from SdkEnumType with the KnownXXX pattern for extensible enums.
acceptance_criteria: Extensible enums → KnownXXX enum + type XXX = string alias
functional.implementation_notes: EnumDeclaration component generates type alias + KnownXXX enum from SdkEnumType. Handles fixed (union of literals) and extensible (base type) patterns. 7 unit tests.

functional.title: Union Type Generation
functional.description: Generate TypeScript union type aliases from SdkUnionType.
acceptance_criteria: SdkUnionType → type XXX = A | B | C
Variant types correctly referenced via refkeys
functional.implementation_notes: UnionDeclaration component generates type alias from SdkUnionType using <For> with joiner for pipe-separated variant types. Uses getTypeExpression() for variant resolution and typeRefkey() for declaration identity. 7 unit tests cover mixed primitives, model variants, JSDoc docs, single-variant edge case, and cross-reference via refkey.

functional.title: JSON Serialization
functional.description: Generate serializer and deserializer functions for all model types used in request/response bodies.
acceptance_criteria: Serializer function per model with Input usage
Deserializer function per model with Output/Exception usage
Property name mapping (client name → wire name and vice versa)
Nested model types call child serializer refkeys
Array types use .map() with child serializer
functional.implementation_notes: JsonSerializer and JsonDeserializer components generate serializer/deserializer functions from SdkModelType. Serializers map client names to wire names, deserializers reverse. Handles nested models (child serializer calls via refkey), arrays (.map() with child serializer), optional properties (null-check ternary guards), and simple passthrough types. Uses Alloy ObjectExpression/ObjectProperty for proper formatting. Integrated into ModelFiles orchestrator with Usage flag filtering (Input for serializers, Output/Exception for deserializers). 15 unit tests.

functional.title: Polymorphic Serialization
functional.description: Generate discriminated union serializers that switch on discriminator property to route to subtype serializers.
acceptance_criteria: Each case routes to subtype serializer/deserializer refkey
Parameter type uses polymorphicTypeRefkey (union type, not base)

functional.title: XML Serialization
functional.description: Generate XML serializer/deserializer functions (4 per type) using metadata-driven approach.
acceptance_criteria: 4 functions per type: serializer, objectSerializer, deserializer, objectDeserializer
Property metadata arrays with XML options
functional.implementation_notes: XML serialization implemented via XmlSerializer, XmlObjectSerializer, XmlDeserializer, XmlObjectDeserializer components. Metadata-driven approach with 4 functions per model. XmlHelpersFile provides static helper functions. 14 unit tests.

functional.title: Operation Generation
functional.description: Generate operation functions with the send/deserialize/public pattern for each API operation.
acceptance_criteria: _sendXXX function builds HTTP request (URL, headers, query, body)
_deserializeXXX function processes response (status check, body parse, headers)
Public xxx function calls send → deserialize
Operations grouped by operation group path
Each group in separate source file under src/api/
functional.notes: Send function (3.2), Deserialize function (3.3), and Public operation function (3.4) are all implemented. Remaining: Operations orchestrator (3.5) to group operations into files.
functional.implementation_notes: All 5 sub-tasks complete: OperationOptionsDeclaration (3.1), SendOperation (3.2), DeserializeOperation (3.3), PublicOperation (3.4), OperationFiles orchestrator (3.5). Operations grouped by path prefix into separate source files under api/.

functional.title: Operation Options
functional.description: Generate TypeScript interfaces for optional operation parameters.
functional.notes: OperationOptionsDeclaration component generates OptionalParams interfaces. Supports LRO updateIntervalInMs, dual-format contentType, JSDoc, and OperationOptions extension.

functional.title: Client Context
functional.description: Generate client context interface and factory function.
acceptance_criteria: API version property for versioned services
Factory function with endpoint, credentials, options parameters
functional.implementation_notes: ClientContextFile generates context interface extending Client, options interface extending ClientOptions, and factory function with endpoint/credential/options handling. Handles URL templates, API version, telemetry.

functional.title: Classical Client Class
functional.description: Generate the user-facing client class wrapping operation-level API.
acceptance_criteria: Operation methods delegating to API functions
Operation group getters for nested groups
functional.implementation_notes: ClassicalClientFile generates class with private _client field, public pipeline property, constructor calling factory function, operation methods delegating to API functions, and operation group getters.

functional.title: Classical Operation Groups
functional.description: Generate operation group interfaces and factory functions for hierarchical client APIs.
functional.implementation_notes: ClassicalOperationGroupFiles generates interface per group level, factory function returning operation methods, nested group properties, and LRO helper methods.

functional.title: LRO Support
functional.description: Support long-running operations with polling and restore capabilities.
acceptance_criteria: RestorePoller helper generated for state restoration
URL template matching for operation routing
functional.implementation_notes: RestorePollerFile generates LRO restore poller helper. PollingHelpersFile provides getLongRunningPoller. Operations wrapped with getLongRunningPoller via PublicOperation component.

functional.title: Paging Support
functional.description: Support paginated operations with async iterators.
functional.implementation_notes: PagingHelpersFile provides buildPagedAsyncIterator, PagedAsyncIterableIterator, PageSettings. Paging operations wrapped in PublicOperation component.

functional.title: Multipart Support
functional.description: Support multipart form data request bodies.
functional.implementation_notes: MultipartSerializer generates multipart form-data serialization. MultipartHelpersFile provides FileContents type and createFilePartDescriptor function. 6 unit tests.

functional.title: Index/Barrel Files
functional.description: Generate proper export structure with root and subpath index files.
acceptance_criteria: Root index.ts exports clients, models, options, helper types
Subpath index.ts in api/, models/, classic/
Serializer functions excluded from public exports
functional.implementation_notes: IndexFiles generates root index.ts, models/index.ts, api/index.ts, classic/index.ts barrel files with selective exports. Internal _prefixed symbols excluded.

functional.title: Static Helper Files
functional.description: Emit static helper source files (serialization utils, collection builders, URL template, etc.).
acceptance_criteria: Each helper has refkey declaration for cross-file reference
Import remapping for Azure vs non-Azure packages
functional.implementation_notes: StaticHelpers orchestrator renders 5 helper files: serializationHelpers.ts, pagingHelpers.ts, pollingHelpers.ts, multipartHelpers.ts, xmlHelpers.ts. Each uses refkeys for cross-file reference.

functional.title: Sample Generation
functional.implementation_notes: SampleFiles generates per-operation sample files in samples-dev/. Handles OAuth2/API key credentials, endpoint env vars, operation calls. 18 unit tests.

functional.title: Logger File
functional.description: Generate logger module for Azure-flavored SDKs.
acceptance_criteria: Package name derived from package details
functional.implementation_notes: LoggerFile component generates logger.ts with createClientLogger() from @azure/logger. Takes packageName prop for the logger namespace. Exports logger via refkey for cross-file references. 7 unit tests cover import generation, export, package name embedding, const declaration, refkey resolution, and legacy output parity.

non_functional.title: Output Parity
non_functional.description: Generated TypeScript code must match legacy emitter output. All 101 scenario tests must pass.

non_functional.title: Performance
non_functional.description: Emitter performance should be comparable to or better than legacy emitter for typical service specs.

non_functional.title: Code Quality
non_functional.description: All components follow Alloy idiomatic patterns. No anti-patterns (string-based refs, manual imports, monolithic code blocks).

non_functional.title: Testability
non_functional.description: Every component is independently testable with the Alloy testing utilities (toRenderTo matcher, TypeSpec test host).

non_functional.title: Extensibility
non_functional.description: Azure-specific features addable via JSX composition without modifying core components.

framework.name: Alloy Framework
framework.description: JSX-based code generation framework. NOT React. Uses JSX purely as structural description language for code output. Provides automatic symbol resolution, cross-file import management, and naming policies.

sdk_model.name: TCGC (TypeSpec Client Generator Core)
sdk_model.description: Provides SdkPackage object model that pre-computes data from TypeSpec definitions. Language-agnostic SDK type system with clients, operations, models, enums, and type metadata.

technical_architecture.data_flow: TypeSpec Input → Compiler → TCGC SdkPackage → Alloy JSX Components → Symbol Tree → TypeScript Files

key_concepts.refkeys: Symbolic reference keys created via refkey(entity, discriminator). Each declaration gets a refkey; references use the same refkey. Alloy resolves cross-file references and auto-generates imports.
key_concepts.code_templates: Tagged template literals (code`...`) that treat interpolated refkeys as live symbolic references. Alloy tracks them and resolves imports automatically.
key_concepts.context_providers: Named contexts via createNamedContext() for dependency injection. Eliminates prop drilling across deeply nested component trees.
key_concepts.external_packages: NPM packages defined via createPackage() with typed symbol accessors. Used in code templates for auto-import from runtime libraries.

test_categories.description: Markdown-based tests ported from legacy emitter. Each .md file contains TypeSpec input and expected TypeScript output blocks. Tests validate output parity between legacy and new emitter.
test_categories.format: H1 = test name, ```tsp = TypeSpec input, ```typescript <type> = expected output

test_categories.description: JSX component tests using Alloy's toRenderTo matcher. Compile TypeSpec snippets, render components, assert output text.
Pure function tests for utilities (refkey helpers, type mapping, etc.).

End-to-end tests ported from legacy emitter's modularIntegration suite. Each test generates a client library from a Spector TypeSpec spec, then exercises the generated client against the Spector mock server to validate runtime behavior. Uses Vitest with globalSetup for server lifecycle.

test_infrastructure.test_host: test/test-host.ts — Tester instance with TypeSpec library imports
test_infrastructure.scenario_harness: test/scenarios/scenarios.spec.ts — MD file parser + assertion engine

unacceptable_differences: Public API surface changes (exported types, function signatures)

tasks.title: Fix array deserialization producing unresolved refkeys
tasks.description: getDeserializationExpression in json-deserializer.tsx produces <Unresolved Symbol> for array element types that pass needsTransformation() but lack a corresponding deserializer declaration. The root cause is inconsistency across 4 parallel predicate systems (needsTransformation, valueTypeHasNamedDeserializerFn, valueTypeHasNamedDeserializer, and guards in getDeserializationExpression). Recommended fix: Approach A — create unified typeHasDeserializerDeclaration/typeHasSerializerDeclaration predicate functions as a single source of truth, and wire them into getDeserializationExpression and getSerializationExpression guards. See docs/design-reviews/serialization-and-options-bugs.md Issue 1 for full design review with 3 approaches analyzed.

tasks.title: Fix deserializer/serializer property keys not matching name policy casing
tasks.description: Deserializer return object keys use raw TCGC prop.name (e.g., Code, Message) instead of name-policy-normalized camelCase (code, message). Model interfaces go through Alloy InterfaceMember which applies camelCase via name policy, but ObjectProperty in deserializers and string accessors in serializers bypass this. Affects json-deserializer.tsx (line 157), json-serializer.tsx (line 157), xml-object-serializer.tsx, and xml-object-deserializer.tsx. Recommended fix: Approach A — use namekey(prop.name) for ObjectProperty names + new normalizePropertyName() utility in name-policy.ts for string template accessors. See docs/design-reviews/serialization-and-options-bugs.md Issue 2 for full design review with 3 approaches analyzed.

tasks.title: Send function uses JSON serializerRefkey for XML body operations
tasks.description:
buildBodyExpression() in send-operation.tsx (line ~834) calls getSerializationExpression() from json-serializer.tsx, which always uses serializerRefkey(type) — the JSON serializer refkey. For operations with XML content type (contentType: 'application/xml'), the body should be serialized with xmlSerializerRefkey(type) instead. XML input models are excluded from JSON serializer generation (model-files.tsx line ~173), so serializerRefkey(type) has no declaration → unresolved symbol in output.

Affected operations (6 unresolved symbols, 54 cascade TS errors):
- api/service/operations.ts _setPropertiesSend: body should use storageServicePropertiesXmlSerializer
- api/service/operations.ts _getUserDelegationKeySend: body should use keyInfoXmlSerializer
- api/blob/operations.ts _setTagsSend: body should use XML serializer for tags
- api/container/operations.ts _setAccessPolicySend: body should use XML serializer for containerAcl
- api/blockBlob/operations.ts _commitBlockListSend: body should use blockLookupListXmlSerializer
- api/blockBlob/operations.ts _querySend: body should use queryRequestXmlSerializer

Fix approach: Make buildBodyExpression() in send-operation.tsx content-type-aware. When the operation's body content type is 'application/xml', use xmlSerializerRefkey(type) from src/utils/refkeys.ts instead of serializerRefkey(type). The XML serializer functions already exist in generated output — they just aren't referenced. The content type is available from the SdkBodyParameter or SdkHttpOperation metadata. Look at how contentType is already extracted in send-operation.tsx for the contentType request option to determine the XML branch.

Key files:
- src/components/send-operation.tsx: buildBodyExpression() (line ~834), getBodyContentType()
- src/components/serialization/json-serializer.tsx: getSerializationExpression() (line ~303)
- src/utils/refkeys.ts: serializerRefkey() vs xmlSerializerRefkey()

Test: After fix, run pnpm build && pnpm test:smoke — the 6 unresolved symbols in operations.ts files should disappear.

tasks.title: Array serializer helpers generated for XML-only types reference non-existent JSON serializers
tasks.description:
The JsonArraySerializer component (src/components/serialization/json-array-record-helpers.tsx line ~60) generates array helper functions like corsRuleArraySerializer() that internally call getSerializationExpression(type.valueType, 'item'), producing serializerRefkey(CorsRule). But CorsRule is an XML-only model that only has corsRuleXmlObjectSerializer, not corsRuleSerializer → unresolved symbol.

Affected array serializers (4 unresolved symbols, 24 cascade TS errors in models/models.ts):
- corsRuleArraySerializer → references missing corsRuleSerializer (only XML exists)
- blobTagArraySerializer → references missing blobTagSerializer (only XML exists)
- signedIdentifierArraySerializer → references missing signedIdentifierSerializer (only XML exists)
- arrowFieldArraySerializer → references missing arrowFieldSerializer (only XML exists)

Fix approach (two options, pick one):

Option A (Recommended): Don't generate JSON array serializers for XML-only types. In model-files.tsx where array/record serializer helpers are generated, filter out types whose element type is XML-only (hasXmlSerialization() from src/utils/xml-detection.ts). These array serializers are dead code anyway because the send functions also reference the wrong serializer (SMOKE-1). XML array serialization is handled inline by XmlObjectSerializer (xml-object-serializer.tsx line ~114) using xmlObjectSerializerRefkey(inner) in the .map() call.

Option B: Make array serializer reference xmlObjectSerializerRefkey when the item type is XML-only. The .map() callback would call xmlObjectSerializerRefkey(itemType) instead of serializerRefkey(itemType).

Key files:
- src/components/serialization/json-array-record-helpers.tsx: JsonArraySerializer component (line ~40-70)
- src/components/model-files.tsx: where array serializer helpers are rendered
- src/utils/xml-detection.ts: hasXmlSerialization() helper

Dependency: This may be resolved as a side effect of SMOKE-1 if the approach also prevents array helper generation for XML types.

Test: After fix, run pnpm build && pnpm test:smoke — the 4 unresolved symbols in models/models.ts should disappear.

tasks.title: Create emit-e2e generation script with spec auto-discovery
tasks.description:
Create `eng/scripts/emit-e2e.ts` — a standalone script that discovers and generates client libraries from all Spector specs.

## What it does
1. Auto-discovers specs by globbing `node_modules/@typespec/http-specs/specs/**/{main,client}.tsp` (prefer client.tsp over main.tsp per directory)
2. Filters specs against a `.testignore` file at `test/e2e/.testignore`
3. Runs `tsp compile` for each spec with configurable parallelism (pLimit based on os.cpus().length)
4. Outputs generated code to `test/e2e/generated/{spec-path}/` (e.g., `test/e2e/generated/authentication/api-key/src/...`)
5. Reports results: X succeeded, Y failed, Z skipped
6. Writes error logs to `temp/emit-e2e-logs/{spec-path}/error.log`

## Key implementation details

### Spec discovery
- Use `globby` or Node fs.glob to find `**/{main,client}.tsp` under the http-specs package
- If both `client.tsp` and `main.tsp` exist in same directory, prefer `client.tsp`
- The http-specs package is at `node_modules/@typespec/http-specs/specs`

### .testignore format
One spec path per line (relative to specs dir). Lines starting with # are comments. Example:
```
# Streaming not supported yet
streaming/jsonl
# Multipart needs work
payload/multipart
```

### tsp compile invocation
For each spec, run:
```bash
npx tsp compile <spec-path> --emit <emitter-path> --output-dir <output-dir>
```
Where:
- `<spec-path>` is the absolute path to the main.tsp or client.tsp file
- `<emitter-path>` is the root of this repo (resolve from import.meta.dirname up to repo root). The emitter is loaded from `dist/` so the project MUST be built first.
- `<output-dir>` is `test/e2e/generated/{relative-spec-path}/`

Pass emitter options via the `--option` flag or via a tspconfig.yaml. The emitter needs these options:
- `generate-metadata: true` (generates package.json, tsconfig.json)
- `flavor: core` (standard non-Azure flavor)

### Parallelism
Use `p-limit` (or similar) to run up to `os.cpus().length` compilations in parallel. Each compilation is a child process via `child_process.execFile` or `execa`.

### CLI interface
Support these flags:
- `--filter <glob>` — only process specs matching this pattern (e.g., `--filter 'type/array'`)
- No flags = process all non-ignored specs

### Error handling
- If a spec fails to compile, log stderr to `temp/emit-e2e-logs/` and continue with remaining specs
- Exit with code 1 if any spec failed
- Print summary at end: 'N/M specs succeeded, K skipped, J failed'

## Reference implementations
- **Primary reference**: `submodules/typespec/packages/http-client-js/eng/scripts/emit-e2e.js` — the reference emitter's generation script. Study its spec discovery (globby for `**/*.client.tsp` or `**/*.main.tsp`), pLimit(4) parallelism, error logging to temp/emit-e2e-logs/, and Prettier post-processing.
- **Legacy reference**: `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/commands/gen-cadl-ranch.js` — uses a hardcoded spec list (`cadl-ranch-list.js`). We do NOT want this approach; prefer auto-discovery.

## Files to create
- `eng/scripts/emit-e2e.ts`
- `test/e2e/.testignore` (initial ignore list for unsupported specs)

## Files to modify
- `package.json` — add script: `"emit:e2e": "tsx eng/scripts/emit-e2e.ts"`
- `.gitignore` — add `test/e2e/generated/`

## Validation
- Run `pnpm build && pnpm emit:e2e` and verify generated code appears in `test/e2e/generated/`
- At least a few specs should generate successfully (e.g., authentication/api-key, routes, type/array)
- Failed specs should produce error logs in `temp/emit-e2e-logs/`

tasks.title: Create Vitest e2e config with Spector server globalSetup
tasks.description:
Create the Vitest configuration and globalSetup/teardown for running Spector integration tests.

## What it does
1. `vitest.config.e2e.ts` — Vitest config that includes only `test/e2e/**/*.test.ts` files
2. `test/e2e/setup/global-setup.ts` — starts the Spector mock server before tests
3. `test/e2e/setup/global-teardown.ts` — stops the server and collects coverage after tests

## vitest.config.e2e.ts
```typescript
import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config';

export default defineConfig({
  test: {
    environment: 'node',
    include: ['test/e2e/**/*.test.ts'],
    globalSetup: ['test/e2e/setup/global-setup.ts'],
    testTimeout: 30_000,
    isolate: false,  // faster: tests share process
  },
});
```

## global-setup.ts
The setup must:
1. Start the Spector mock server using `tsp-spector server start`
2. Pass the specs path: `node_modules/@typespec/http-specs/specs`
3. Use port 3002 (matches legacy convention, avoids conflicts with dev servers)
4. Enable coverage tracking: `--coverageFile temp/spector-coverage.json`
5. Poll for server readiness by making HTTP GET requests to a known endpoint
6. The readiness endpoint: `http://localhost:3002/routes/in-interface/fixed` should return 204
7. Retry up to 30 times with 1s delay between retries
8. Export the `setup()` and `teardown()` functions as Vitest globalSetup expects

### Server start command
```bash
npx tsp-spector server start node_modules/@typespec/http-specs/specs --port 3002 --coverageFile temp/spector-coverage.json
```

### Server stop command (in teardown)
```bash
npx tsp-spector server stop --port 3002
```

## Reference implementations
- **Primary reference**: `submodules/typespec/packages/http-client-js/eng/scripts/run-e2e-tests.js` — see `waitForServer()` function (polls with retries + delay), server spawn, and cleanup logic.
- **Spector CLI**: `submodules/typespec/packages/spector/src/cli/cli.ts` — the `server start` command starts in background, `server stop` stops it. Default port is 3000 but we use 3002.

## Files to create
- `vitest.config.e2e.ts`
- `test/e2e/setup/global-setup.ts`

## Files to modify
- `package.json` — add scripts:
  - `"test:e2e": "pnpm emit:e2e && vitest run --config vitest.config.e2e.ts"`
  - `"test:e2e:only": "vitest run --config vitest.config.e2e.ts"` (skip generation)

## Validation
- Run `pnpm test:e2e:only` with no test files yet — it should start the server, find no tests, and stop cleanly
- Verify the server starts on port 3002 and responds to health checks
- Verify the server stops cleanly in teardown
- Verify `temp/spector-coverage.json` is created

tasks.title: Port first Spector test as proof-of-concept: authentication/api-key
Port authentication and server Spector tests
tasks.description:
Port the authentication and server-related Spector integration tests.

## Tests to port (7 test files)

### Authentication tests
| Legacy test file | Spector spec | New test path |
|---|---|---|
| `authApiKey.spec.ts` | `authentication/api-key` | Already done in SPECTOR-3 |
| `authHttpCustom.spec.ts` | `authentication/http/custom` | `test/e2e/http/authentication/http/custom/main.test.ts` |
| `authOauth2.spec.ts` | `authentication/oauth2` | `test/e2e/http/authentication/oauth2/main.test.ts` |
| `authUnion.spec.ts` | `authentication/union` | `test/e2e/http/authentication/union/main.test.ts` |

### Server tests
| Legacy test file | Spector spec | New test path |
|---|---|---|
| `server.spec.ts` | `server/path/single`, `server/path/multiple`, `server/versions/versioned`, `server/versions/not-versioned` | `test/e2e/http/server/path/single/main.test.ts`, etc. |
| `serverEndpointNotDefined.spec.ts` | `server/endpoint/not-defined` | `test/e2e/http/server/endpoint/not-defined/main.test.ts` |

## Porting pattern
For each test:
1. Read the legacy test in `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/`
2. Read the Spector spec mockapi.ts in `submodules/typespec/packages/http-specs/specs/` to understand expected behavior
3. Optionally reference the test from `submodules/typespec/packages/http-client-js/test/e2e/http/` as a secondary reference
4. Write the new test using Vitest (`describe`, `it`, `expect`, `beforeEach`)
5. Import the generated client from `../../../generated/{spec-path}/src/index.js` (adjust relative depth as needed)
6. Use `endpoint: 'http://localhost:3002'` and `allowInsecureConnection: true`

## Key differences from legacy tests
- Vitest instead of Mocha + Chai
- `expect(result).toBeUndefined()` instead of `assert.isUndefined(result)`
- `expect(result).toBe(value)` instead of `assert.strictEqual(result, value)`
- `expect(result).toEqual(obj)` instead of `assert.deepEqual(result, obj)`
- `expect(() => ...).rejects.toThrow()` for error assertions

## Validation
Run `pnpm build && pnpm test:e2e` and verify all ported tests pass.

tasks.title: Port type system Spector tests (type/array, type/dictionary, type/enum, type/model, type/property, type/scalar, type/union)
tasks.description:
Port all type-system related Spector integration tests. This is the largest batch (~15 test files).

## Tests to port

| Legacy test file | Spector spec | New test path |
|---|---|---|
| `arrayItemTypes.spec.ts` | `type/array` | `test/e2e/http/type/array/main.test.ts` |
| `dictionaryTypes.spec.ts` | `type/dictionary` | `test/e2e/http/type/dictionary/main.test.ts` |
| `extensibleEnums.spec.ts` | `type/enum/extensible` | `test/e2e/http/type/enum/extensible/main.test.ts` |
| `fixedEnums.spec.ts` | `type/enum/fixed` | `test/e2e/http/type/enum/fixed/main.test.ts` |
| `modelsEmpty.spec.ts` | `type/model/empty` | `test/e2e/http/type/model/empty/main.test.ts` |
| `modelInheritanceEnumDiscriminator.spec.ts` | `type/model/inheritance/enum-discriminator` | `test/e2e/http/type/model/inheritance/enum-discriminator/main.test.ts` |
| `modelInheritanceNestedDiscriminator.spec.ts` | `type/model/inheritance/nested-discriminator` | `test/e2e/http/type/model/inheritance/nested-discriminator/main.test.ts` |
| `modelInheritanceNotDiscriminated.spec.ts` | `type/model/inheritance/not-discriminated` | `test/e2e/http/type/model/inheritance/not-discriminated/main.test.ts` |
| `modelInheritanceRecursive.spec.ts` | `type/model/inheritance/recursive` | `test/e2e/http/type/model/inheritance/recursive/main.test.ts` |
| `modelInheritanceSingleDiscriminator.spec.ts` | `type/model/inheritance/single-discriminator` | `test/e2e/http/type/model/inheritance/single-discriminator/main.test.ts` |
| `modelPropertyAdditional.spec.ts` | `type/property/additional-properties` | `test/e2e/http/type/property/additional-properties/main.test.ts` |
| `modelPropertyType.spec.ts` | `type/property/value-types` | `test/e2e/http/type/property/value-types/main.test.ts` |
| `nullableProperties.spec.ts` | `type/property/nullable` | `test/e2e/http/type/property/nullable/main.test.ts` |
| `optionalProperties.spec.ts` | `type/property/optionality` | `test/e2e/http/type/property/optionality/main.test.ts` |
| `scalar.spec.ts` | `type/scalar` | `test/e2e/http/type/scalar/main.test.ts` |
| `unions.spec.ts` | `type/union` | `test/e2e/http/type/union/main.test.ts` |
| `usage.spec.ts` | `type/model/usage` | `test/e2e/http/type/model/usage/main.test.ts` |

## Porting guidelines
Same pattern as SPECTOR-4. For each test:
1. Read legacy test from `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/`
2. Read mockapi.ts from `submodules/typespec/packages/http-specs/specs/` for expected behavior
3. Reference test from `submodules/typespec/packages/http-client-js/test/e2e/http/` if available
4. Write Vitest test at the path in the table above
5. Import generated client from relative path to `test/e2e/generated/{spec-path}/src/index.js`

## Complex tests to watch out for
- **modelInheritanceSingleDiscriminator**: Has complex discriminated union assertions with `assert.deepEqual`. Convert carefully to `expect().toEqual()`.
- **modelPropertyType**: Very large test file with many property type sub-tests. May have 50+ test cases.
- **modelPropertyAdditional**: Tests additional properties (open models). Watch for spread operator and dynamic property handling.

## Validation
Run `pnpm build && pnpm test:e2e` — all type tests should pass.

tasks.title: Port encode and serialization Spector tests
tasks.description:
Port all encoding and serialization related Spector integration tests (6 test files).

## Tests to port

| Legacy test file | Spector spec | New test path |
|---|---|---|
| `encodeArray.spec.ts` | `encode/array` | `test/e2e/http/encode/array/main.test.ts` |
| `encodeBytes.spec.ts` | `encode/bytes` | `test/e2e/http/encode/bytes/main.test.ts` |
| `encodeDatetime.spec.ts` | `encode/datetime` | `test/e2e/http/encode/datetime/main.test.ts` |
| `encodeDuration.spec.ts` | `encode/duration` | `test/e2e/http/encode/duration/main.test.ts` |
| `encodeNumeric.spec.ts` | `encode/numeric` | `test/e2e/http/encode/numeric/main.test.ts` |
| `serializationEncodeNameJson.spec.ts` | `serialization/encoded-name/json` | `test/e2e/http/serialization/encoded-name/json/main.test.ts` |

## Porting guidelines
Same pattern as SPECTOR-4. These tests validate that the emitter correctly handles:
- Byte encoding (base64, base64url, hex)
- Datetime encoding (rfc3339, rfc7231, unixTimestamp)
- Duration encoding (ISO 8601, seconds, float seconds)
- Numeric encoding (safeint, uint8, string encoding for large ints)
- Array encoding (CSV, SSV, TSV, pipes in headers/query)
- JSON property name encoding (@encodedName)

## Reference files
- Legacy tests: `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/encode*.spec.ts`
- Spector specs: `submodules/typespec/packages/http-specs/specs/encode/*/mockapi.ts`
- Reference tests: `submodules/typespec/packages/http-client-js/test/e2e/http/encode/*/main.test.ts`

## Validation
Run `pnpm build && pnpm test:e2e` — all encode/serialization tests should pass.

tasks.title: Port parameters, payload, and routes Spector tests
tasks.description:
Port parameters, payload, and routing Spector integration tests (9 test files).

## Tests to port

### Parameters
| Legacy test file | Spector spec | New test path |
|---|---|---|
| `basicParameters.spec.ts` | `parameters/basic` | `test/e2e/http/parameters/basic/main.test.ts` |
| `bodyOptionality.spec.ts` | `parameters/body-optionality` | `test/e2e/http/parameters/body-optionality/main.test.ts` |
| `collectionFormat.spec.ts` | `parameters/collection-format` | `test/e2e/http/parameters/collection-format/main.test.ts` |
| `spreadParameters.spec.ts` | `parameters/spread` | `test/e2e/http/parameters/spread/main.test.ts` |

### Payload
| Legacy test file | Spector spec | New test path |
|---|---|---|
| `payloadContentNegotiation.spec.ts` | `payload/content-negotiation` | `test/e2e/http/payload/content-negotiation/main.test.ts` |
| `payloadMediaType.spec.ts` | `payload/media-type` | `test/e2e/http/payload/media-type/main.test.ts` |
| `payloadMultipart.spec.ts` | `payload/multipart` | `test/e2e/http/payload/multipart/main.test.ts` |
| `payloadPageable.spec.ts` | `payload/pageable` | `test/e2e/http/payload/pageable/main.test.ts` |

### Routes
| Legacy test file | Spector spec | New test path |
|---|---|---|
| `routes.spec.ts` | `routes` | `test/e2e/http/routes/main.test.ts` |

## Notes
- `payloadMultipart` is complex — uses `fs.createReadStream()` for file uploads. Tests need access to test assets. Create `test/e2e/assets/` with image.jpg and image.png files, or reference them from the existing spector spec assets.
- `payloadPageable` tests pagination. Verify the generated client has paging helpers.
- `routes` is a comprehensive test with many sub-tests for path/query expansion modes.
- `spreadParameters` tests TypeSpec spread operator for parameter destructuring.

## Reference files
- Legacy tests: `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/`
- Spector specs: `submodules/typespec/packages/http-specs/specs/`
- Reference tests: `submodules/typespec/packages/http-client-js/test/e2e/http/`

## Validation
Run `pnpm build && pnpm test:e2e` — all parameter/payload/routes tests should pass.

tasks.title: Port versioning and special Spector tests
tasks.description:
Port versioning, special-words, and special-headers Spector integration tests (10 test files).

## Tests to port

### Versioning
| Legacy test file | Spector spec | New test path |
|---|---|---|
| `versioningAdded.spec.ts` | `versioning/added` | `test/e2e/http/versioning/added/main.test.ts` |
| `versioningMadeOptional.spec.ts` | `versioning/madeOptional` | `test/e2e/http/versioning/madeOptional/main.test.ts` |
| `versioningRemovedV1.spec.ts` | `versioning/removed` (v1 config) | `test/e2e/http/versioning/removed/v1/main.test.ts` |
| `versioningRemovedV2.spec.ts` | `versioning/removed` (v2 config) | `test/e2e/http/versioning/removed/v2/main.test.ts` |
| `versioningRemovedV2Preview.spec.ts` | `versioning/removed` (v2preview config) | `test/e2e/http/versioning/removed/v2preview/main.test.ts` |
| `versioningRenamedFrom.spec.ts` | `versioning/renamedFrom` | `test/e2e/http/versioning/renamedFrom/main.test.ts` |
| `versioningReturnTypeChangedFrom.spec.ts` | `versioning/returnTypeChangedFrom` | `test/e2e/http/versioning/returnTypeChangedFrom/main.test.ts` |
| `versioningTypeChangedFrom.spec.ts` | `versioning/typeChangedFrom` | `test/e2e/http/versioning/typeChangedFrom/main.test.ts` |

### Special
| Legacy test file | Spector spec | New test path |
|---|---|---|
| `specialWords.spec.ts` | `special-words` | `test/e2e/http/special-words/main.test.ts` |
| `headersRepeatability.spec.ts` | `special-headers/repeatability` | `test/e2e/http/special-headers/repeatability/main.test.ts` |

## Notes on versioning tests
The `versioning/removed` spec generates 3 different client variants based on version configuration:
- v1: Only the operations/types from version 1
- v2: Operations/types up to version 2 (v1 removed items are gone)
- v2preview: Preview version with additional preview operations

In the legacy emitter, these are 3 separate entries in cadl-ranch-list.js with different outputPaths but the same inputPath. In the new emitter, the generation script needs to compile the same spec 3 times with different version options. This may require special handling in the emit-e2e script or version-specific tspconfig.yaml files.

## Reference files
- Legacy tests: `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/versioning*.spec.ts`, `specialWords.spec.ts`, `headersRepeatability.spec.ts`
- Legacy cadl-ranch-list.js: Shows how versioning/removed is split into 3 entries with different outputPaths
- Spector specs: `submodules/typespec/packages/http-specs/specs/versioning/`, `special-words/`, `special-headers/`

## Validation
Run `pnpm build && pnpm test:e2e` — all versioning and special tests should pass.

tasks.title: Add e2e test CI integration and coverage reporting
tasks.description:
Integrate the Spector e2e tests into the CI pipeline and set up coverage reporting.

## What to do
1. Add a CI step that runs `pnpm build && pnpm test:e2e` in the existing CI workflow
2. Ensure the Spector coverage file (`temp/spector-coverage.json`) is captured as a CI artifact
3. Add a script to parse and summarize coverage: which Spector scenarios are covered vs not

## Coverage reporting
After tests complete, the Spector server writes coverage data to `temp/spector-coverage.json`. This file tracks which mock API scenarios were hit during testing.

Create `eng/scripts/calculate-coverage.ts` that:
1. Reads `temp/spector-coverage.json`
2. Prints a summary: N/M scenarios covered
3. Lists uncovered scenarios

## Reference
- `submodules/typespec/packages/http-client-js/eng/scripts/calculate-coverage.js` — reference implementation
- Spector coverage format: `CoverageReport[]` from `@typespec/spector`

## Package.json script
Add: `"test:e2e:ci": "pnpm build && pnpm test:e2e"`

## Validation
- CI runs e2e tests successfully
- Coverage artifact is captured

tasks.title: Fix clientInitialization header params reading from context instead of options
tasks.description: 3 scenario tests fail because header parameters with clientInitialization are generated as context["param"] instead of options?.param. Affected tests: (1) apiVersion/apiVersionAsFixedEnum - generates context.apiVersion instead of options?.apiVersion, (2-3) headerParam/headerParamWithClientInitialization (optional + required) - generates context["$expand"] instead of options?.$expand. The send function should read these from options, not from context.

tasks.title: Fix XML object serializer adding unnecessary null guards around array .map()
tasks.description: Scenario test xml/xmlArrayItemTypes fails because the XML object serializer wraps array .map() calls with !item["prop"] ? item["prop"] : item["prop"]?.map(...) instead of just item["prop"]?.map(...). The extra null-guard pattern is not expected by the legacy emitter output. Affected test: payload/scenarios.test.ts > xml/xmlArrayItemTypes.md > XML Arrays with Typed Items > blockLookupListXmlObjectSerializer.

tasks.title: Fix pagingHelpers.ts static helper compilation errors
tasks.description: The generated pagingHelpers.ts has TypeScript compilation errors: (1) 'Cannot find name client' on lines 39-40 — likely a missing parameter or wrong variable reference in buildPagedAsyncIterator, (2) '[Symbol.asyncIterator]' does not exist in type 'PagedAsyncIterableIterator<TElement>' — the interface is missing the async iterator symbol. These errors prevent the smoke test from passing.

tasks.title: Fix pollingHelpers.ts static helper compilation errors
tasks.description: The generated pollingHelpers.ts has TypeScript compilation errors: (1) Module '@azure-rest/core-client' has no exported member 'RestError' — wrong import source for RestError, (2) 'Cannot find name TResult, did you mean TResult_1' — type parameter naming issue in generic function. These errors prevent the smoke test from passing.

tasks.title: Fix operations accessing non-existent version/apiVersion on options/client
tasks.description: Generated operations access 'options?.version' for the x-ms-version header, but the options interfaces don't have a 'version' property. Also 'client.apiVersion' is accessed but Client type doesn't have it. The emitter needs to correctly resolve where apiVersion comes from (likely the client context, not individual options). Affects all operations in service/, container/, pageBlob/, blockBlob/, appendBlob/, blob/.

tasks.title: Fix operations using wrong property casing for option params
tasks.description: Generated operations access option properties with wrong casing: 'BlobTagsString' should be 'blobTagsString', 'PreventEncryptionScopeOverride' should be 'preventEncryptionScopeOverride', 'transactionalContentMD5' should be 'transactionalContentMd5'. The operation send function uses the original wire name instead of the camelCase name from the options interface. This is a name policy consistency issue between model interfaces and operation code.

tasks.title: Fix classic wrapper return types for paged operations
tasks.description: Classic wrapper methods for paged operations are typed as returning Promise<T[]> but the underlying API operations return PagedAsyncIterableIterator<T>. Affects container/index.ts (listBlobs, listBlobHierarchySegment) and service/index.ts (listContainers). The classic wrapper needs to propagate the correct paged return type.

tasks.title: Fix buildCsvCollection called with enum array types
tasks.description: buildCsvCollection is called with FilterBlobsIncludeItem[] | undefined and ListBlobsIncludeItem[] | undefined, but its signature only accepts string[] | number[]. The enum array types are not compatible. Either buildCsvCollection needs to accept broader types, or the enum arrays need to be cast/mapped to strings before passing.

phases_metadata.name: Project Foundation & Infrastructure
phases_metadata.description: Set up dependencies, emitter entry point, context system, refkey helpers, external packages, and test infrastructure.

phases_metadata.name: Models & Type Declarations
phases_metadata.description: Generate TypeScript interfaces, enums, type aliases, and union types from TCGC types.

phases_metadata.name: Serialization & Deserialization
phases_metadata.description: Generate JSON and XML serializer/deserializer functions for all model types.

phases_metadata.name: Operations
phases_metadata.description: Generate operation functions with send/deserialize pattern.

phases_metadata.name: Client Infrastructure
phases_metadata.description: Generate client context interface, factory function, and logger.

phases_metadata.name: Classical Client
phases_metadata.description: Generate user-facing client class and operation groups.

phases_metadata.name: Advanced Features
phases_metadata.description: Implement LRO restore, multipart, and sample generation.

phases_metadata.name: Azure Flavor Extension
phases_metadata.description: Design and implement Azure-specific extensions via composition.

phases_metadata.name: Test Porting & Validation
phases_metadata.description: Port all 101 scenario tests and validate output parity.

phases_metadata.name: Validation Fixes — Remaining 124 Test Failures
phases_metadata.description: Tasks to fix the remaining 124 scenario test failures identified during validation. Categorized by root cause. Total: 124 failures across 9 categories.

phases_metadata.name: Scenario Test Parity — Audit Findings
phases_metadata.description: Tasks derived from the scenario test audit comparing 102 test files between the new Alloy emitter and the legacy autorest.typescript emitter. Each task represents a category of semantic differences that must be resolved to achieve output parity.

phases_metadata.title: Scenario Test Parity — Root Cause Fixes
phases_metadata.description: Address all semantic differences identified by the scenario audit, recategorized by root cause. Each task maps to a specific code location and a single fix. 36 tasks total.
phases_metadata.name: Remaining Corrections
Flavor Parity — Match Legacy Emitter Flavor Behavior
phases_metadata.description: Ensure the new emitter correctly handles the 'flavor' option ('core' vs 'azure') with full parity to the legacy emitter. Core flavor should use @typespec/ts-http-runtime and NOT generate Azure-specific features (LRO, paging, logger, DefaultAzureCredential). Azure flavor should use Azure SDK packages. No 'branded' option support. Flavor must be set explicitly or not at all (defaulting to 'core').

phases_metadata.name: Spector Integration Tests — End-to-End Validation
phases_metadata.description: Set up infrastructure to generate client libraries from Spector specs, run them against the Spector mock server, and validate end-to-end behavior. Uses Approach C (Hybrid): separate generation script + Vitest globalSetup for server lifecycle. This replaces the legacy emitter's modularIntegration test suite.

scenario_audit_metadata.description: Comprehensive audit of semantic differences between NEW (http-client-js) and LEGACY (autorest.typescript) scenario test expectations. Conducted by comparing all 91 matched .md files across both test suites. The LEGACY emitter output is the source of truth - all regression tasks specify that the expected state matches the legacy test.

intentional_changes.title: INTENTIONAL: Runtime Package Migration (@azure-rest/core-client → @typespec/ts-http-runtime)
intentional_changes.description: The new emitter targets @typespec/ts-http-runtime instead of @azure-rest/core-client. This is the intended design direction. No action needed unless Azure flavor detection requires @azure-rest/core-client.

intentional_changes.title: INTENTIONAL: Type-Only Import Modifiers (import type)
intentional_changes.description: The new emitter adds the type keyword to type-only imports per TypeScript best practices. This is correct behavior.

intentional_changes.title: INTENTIONAL: expandUrlTemplate Import from Runtime
intentional_changes.description: expandUrlTemplate utility is now imported from the runtime package instead of a static-helpers path. This is part of the runtime consolidation.

risks.title: Output Parity Failures
risks.description: Generated code may differ from legacy emitter in ways that break existing Azure SDK consumers.
risks.mitigation: Run scenario tests incrementally during each phase. Accept only cosmetic differences (import ordering, whitespace).

risks.title: Alloy Version Incompatibilities
risks.description: Alloy API changes between versions may require migration work.
risks.mitigation: Pin to @alloy-js/*@0.22.0. Update deliberately with version bumps.

risks.title: TCGC API Surface Changes
risks.description: TCGC types may evolve during development, requiring adaptation.
risks.mitigation: Pin TCGC version. Monitor TCGC releases and adapt incrementally.

risks.title: Self-Import Bugs
risks.description: Separate SourceFile elements for related declarations (types + serializers) cause Alloy to generate self-imports.
risks.mitigation: Always unify declarations and serializers in same SourceFile via ModelFiles pattern.

risks.title: Static Helper Loading Complexity
risks.description: 21 helper files with varied formats need proper refkey registration and source loading.
risks.mitigation: Start with refkey declarations. Load helper sources as-is. Test each helper independently.

risks.title: Performance Regression
risks.description: Alloy framework overhead may slow code generation for very large service specs.
risks.mitigation: Benchmark against legacy emitter on large specs. Alloy is optimized for codegen.
